title: Yavin (deity)
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Deity |image=[[File:Yavin_Prime_bis.png]] |name=Yavin
{{C|The gas giant Yavin, which the Massassi viewed as a deity, depicted in the sky of Yavin 4}} |religion=[[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] beliefs''[[Before the Storm]]'' |pantheon= |type= |powers= |worship= |artifacts= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns= |form= |first= |area=[[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] |affiliation=[[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] }} {{Quote|Yavin was both a beautiful and a terrible god to the Massassi. It lifted their eyes to the heavens, but made their hearts small and fearful.|Streen|}} '''Yavin''' was the [[Deity/Legends|god]] of the [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]], the warrior caste of the [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. [[Gender/Legends|He]] was represented by the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin Prime]] that filled the skies of its [[Yavin 4/Legends|fourth]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]]. In the [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] warriors, the [[Color/Legends|red]] god was awe-inspiring because of his all-dominating presence in the firmament. When the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] ordered the Massassi to assemble [[Massassi Temple|monuments]], they found their own purpose in that command. In hopes of becoming like gods themselves, the Massassi built the temples in a vain attempt to reach their deity. Yavin was recorded in the [[Books of Massassi]], a collection of sacred texts. ==History== {{Quote|They gathered these stones for the Sith in a vain effort to touch the face of their god.|Luke Skywalker|}} [[File:Massassi_architects.png|thumb|left|160px|The Massassi architects on Yavin 4]] Around [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] was defeated at a [[Second battle of Korriban|battle]] of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. Sadow, accompanied by his remaining followers in a single [[Starship/Legends|starship]] crewed by [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] warriors, went into self-imposed [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]] on the remote [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], the fourth [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin Prime]].[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 5]] The Massassi who followed Naga Sadow to Yavin 4 came to regard Yavin Prime, the [[Color/Legends|red]] [[Planet/Legends|orb]] that filled the sky, as a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Deity/Legends|deity]] that was fascinating and terrifying at the same [[Time/Legends|time]]. Under Yavin's overwhelming presence above them, the warriors felt insignificant and frightened. When Sadow ordered his Massassi [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] to clear the ground and build [[Massassi Temple|temples]] on Yavin 4, they took it as an opportunity to reach the heavenly realm of Yavin and achieve godly status themselves. The religious experience of the Massassi with Yavin was described in the [[Books of Massassi]], a series of engraved tablets. [[Standard year/Legends|Millennia]] later, the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] from [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] [[Jedi Praxeum|Praxeum]] unearthed the Books from a collapsed underground chamber in the jungle. In [[16 ABY/Legends|16 ABY]], the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Streen]] translated the tablets and rediscovered the legend of Yavin. ==Behind the scenes== The red god Yavin was only briefly mentioned in ''[[Before the Storm]]'', a [[1996]] novel written by [[Michael P. Kube-McDowell]]. ==Appearances== *''[[Before the Storm]]'' {{Mo}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Deities]] [[Category:Sith deities]]
